2026-04-26T13:54:09.7882377Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-26T13:54:09.7882789Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-26T13:54:09.7883140Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-26T13:54:09.7883434Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-04-26T13:55:01.4157271Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-26T13:54:47.102198232Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":26676,\"title\":\"[Feature Request] Add data-plane client library for Azure Load Testing (azure-developer-loadtesting)\",\"body\":\"## Feature Request\\n\\n  The Go SDK currently only provides a management-plane library for Azure Load Testing (`armloadtesting`), which supports creating and managing Load Testing resources. However, there is no data-plane client library\\n  for interacting with individual test definitions, test runs, and test results.\\n\\n  Data-plane client libraries already exist for other languages:\\n  - **Python**: `azure-developer-loadtesting`\\n  - **.NET**: `Azure.Developer.LoadTesting`\\n  - **JavaScript**: `@azure-rest/load-testing`\\n\\n  ## Use Case\\n\\n  We need to programmatically list and inspect test definitions within a Load Testing account (e.g., `GET /tests`) to read configuration properties like `publicIPDisabled` and `subnetId` that are only available\\n  through the data-plane API.\\n\\n  Currently, we work around this by making raw HTTP calls to the data-plane endpoint using the `DataPlaneURI` from the ARM resource and manually acquiring tokens with scope\\n  `https://cnt-prod.loadtesting.azure.com/.default`. A native Go client would provide type safety, built-in authentication, and consistency with the other language SDKs.\\n\\n  ## API Reference\\n\\n  - Data-plane REST API: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/loadtesting/dataplane(2022-11-01)/load-test-administration\\n  - ARM SDK (existing): https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/resourcemanager/loadtesting/armloadtesting\\n\\n  ## Expected Package\\n\\n  `github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/developer/loadtesting` (following the Python naming convention)\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/issues/26676\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"itakedem\",\"id\":61678846,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/itakedem\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61678846?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"labels\":[\"question\",\"customer-reported\",\"needs-triage\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-26T13:53:22Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-26T13:53:53Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-26T13:55:01.4173491Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-26T13:54:47.347171266Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":26676,\"title\":\"[Feature Request] Add data-plane client library for Azure Load Testing (azure-developer-loadtesting)\",\"body\":\"## Feature Request\\n\\n  The Go SDK currently only provides a management-plane library for Azure Load Testing (`armloadtesting`), which supports creating and managing Load Testing resources. However, there is no data-plane client library\\n  for interacting with individual test definitions, test runs, and test results.\\n\\n  Data-plane client libraries already exist for other languages:\\n  - **Python**: `azure-developer-loadtesting`\\n  - **.NET**: `Azure.Developer.LoadTesting`\\n  - **JavaScript**: `@azure-rest/load-testing`\\n\\n  ## Use Case\\n\\n  We need to programmatically list and inspect test definitions within a Load Testing account (e.g., `GET /tests`) to read configuration properties like `publicIPDisabled` and `subnetId` that are only available\\n  through the data-plane API.\\n\\n  Currently, we work around this by making raw HTTP calls to the data-plane endpoint using the `DataPlaneURI` from the ARM resource and manually acquiring tokens with scope\\n  `https://cnt-prod.loadtesting.azure.com/.default`.
